title: These were Boston’s most popular dog names in 2025
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Luna was the most popular dog name in Boston in 2025, with 137 registered.
Labrador retrievers were the most common breed, with 1,961 registered in Boston last year.
Boston dog owners must license pets over six months old by March 31 to avoid a $50 fine.

title: Another wintry mix? Here’s what meteorologists are saying about the timing and impacts.
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A wintry mix will affect southern New England from Thursday night into Friday morning, impacting commutes.
Greatest ice risk is in southern Worcester Hills and Berkshires, with significant sleet expected further north.
Expect rain, freezing rain, sleet, and snow; roads will be slippery, especially in areas north of the Pike.

title: One of the best brewery tours in America is in New England, according to USA Today readers
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Allagash Brewing Co. in Portland, Maine ranked No. 6 in USA Today's 10Best Readers’ Choice Awards for brewery tours.
Allagash Brewing offers tours like the 30-minute Taste of Allagash and the 1-hour Signature Beer & Barrels Experience.
Schilling Beer Co. in N.H. and Hill Farmstead Brewery in Vt. also recognized by USA Today for brewpubs and beer gardens.

title: Foul play suspected after human remains found in Shirley, police say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Human remains were found near the Maritime Veterans Memorial Bridge in Shirley on Wednesday evening.
Preliminary investigation suggests foul play, and police are collaborating with State Police detectives and a dive team.
Increased police presence is expected near the Veterans Memorial Bridge and Shaker Road in Shirley.

title: Boston community grapples with fear and protest after U.S.-Israeli strike in Iran
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston community holds protests in response to U.S.-Israeli strike in Iran killing Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
Local Iranian-Americans express anxiety over family safety and destruction in Iran following the escalation.
Massachusetts politicians and activists criticize the legality and leadership of U.S. involvement in the conflict.

title: 5 best new restaurant openings and dining updates in Boston (March 5, 2026)
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: LPM Restaurant will open its first Northeast location in Boston's Back Bay in summer 2026.
Cafe Noodo opens next to TD Garden, offering hand-pulled noodle soups and customizable boba tea.
Call Me Honey replaces Curio Coffee in Cambridge, serving coffee and liège waffles.

title: 3 reasons for optimism and 3 causes for concern as the Red Sox approach a new season
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Roman Anthony and Ceddanne Rafaela lead a promising young Red Sox core, boosting hopes for a strong season.
Veteran additions Garrett Crochet and Ranger Suárez strengthen the Red Sox rotation, providing stability and experience.
Concerns include Marcelo Mayer's recovery from wrist surgery and the impact of the World Baseball Classic on player readiness.

title: Boston police seeking suspect in armed robbery
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police are seeking a suspect in an armed robbery on January 22 in Dorchester.
The suspect allegedly robbed multiple victims at gunpoint near Adams Street and Whitten Street.
Police urge the public to provide information via detectives at 617-343-4335 or CrimeStoppers at 1-800-494-TIPS.

title: No license yet for World Cup games as Foxborough asks for public safety funding up front
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Foxborough officials demand $7.8 million in public safety funding up front for World Cup games at Gillette Stadium.
Boston FIFA World Cup 2026 host committee proposed payment within two days post-games, not up front, causing tension.
Town considers withholding entertainment license for June-July games; funding pledged by Kraft Sports if grants fall through.

title: Wu to host first-ever ‘office hours’ for residents to speak with her directly
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu will hold office hours in Roxbury for residents to discuss city issues on Thursday evening.
City department staff will be present to answer questions from residents and provide services like voting registration.
The event is the first of its kind in Boston, as announced during Wu's inaugural address in January.
